sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, name CLOB DEFAULT NULL, value DECIMAL(10, 2));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 100.50), (2, 'Bob', NULL), (3, 'Charlie', 200.00), (4, '', 0.00);
SELECT sys.group_concat(name, ', ') FROM test_data WHERE value <> 200.00 GROUP BY (CASE WHEN value < 100.00 THEN 1 ELSE 2 END);
SELECT id, sys.group_concat(name, '|') AS concatenated_names FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING sys.group_concat(name, ', ') <> '';
SELECT name FROM (SELECT sys.group_concat(name, '-') AS combined_names FROM test_data GROUP BY (id <> 3)) subquery WHERE combined_names <> 'Alice-Bob';
SELECT sys.group_concat(name, ', ') FROM (SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS filtered_data WHERE id <> 2;

SELECT DISTINCT sys.group_concat(name, '/') AS people_in_city FROM (SELECT d1.name, d2.value FROM test_data d1 JOIN test_data d2 ON d1.id <> d2.id WHERE d1.value >= 100.00) AS joined_data GROUP BY value HAVING sys.group_concat(name, '|') <> '';
SELECT sys.group_concat(name, ' & ') FROM (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM test_data WHERE value <> 0.00) AS unique_names WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL);
